stats.h(3) Library Functions Manual stats.h(3)

NAME

stats.h - Server performance statistics tracking.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct server_stats_t
Server performance statistics structure.

Functions


void * stats_logger_thread (void *arg)
Main statistics collection and reporting thread function. int stats_init (void)
Initialize the stats mutex. void stats_cleanup (void)
Cleanup the stats mutex. void update_server_stats (void)
Update global server statistics (placeholder). void log_server_stats (void)
Log comprehensive server statistics summary.

Variables


server_stats_t g_stats
Global server statistics structure. mutex_t g_stats_mutex
Mutex protecting global server statistics.

Detailed Description

Server performance statistics tracking.

This header provides server-side performance statistics tracking including frame capture/send rates, bandwidth metrics, and performance counters.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Definition in file stats.h.

Function Documentation

void log_server_stats (void )

Log comprehensive server statistics summary. Outputs a formatted summary of server performance statistics including frame processing rates, throughput metrics, and system health indicators. This function provides operational visibility into server performance.

STATISTICS REPORTED:

FRAME PROCESSING METRICS:

  • frames_captured: Total frames received from all clients
  • frames_sent: Total ASCII frames delivered to all clients
  • frames_dropped: Frames lost due to overload or errors

PERFORMANCE INDICATORS:

  • avg_capture_fps: Moving average of frame capture rate
  • avg_send_fps: Moving average of frame delivery rate

THREAD SAFETY:

  • Acquires g_stats_mutex for atomic statistics snapshot
  • Prevents inconsistent reporting during concurrent updates
  • Minimal lock hold time (only during data copy)

USAGE SCENARIOS:

  • Periodic performance reporting
  • Debug information during troubleshooting
  • System health monitoring
  • Performance trend analysis

OUTPUT FORMAT:

  • Human-readable format suitable for log analysis
  • Structured data for automated monitoring
  • Consistent formatting for operational visibility

Note

Function provides thread-safe access to global statistics

Statistics snapshot is atomic to ensure consistency

See also

update_server_stats() For statistics update implementation

Definition at line 554 of file stats.c.

554                             {
555   // Check if stats mutex is initialized (safe in debug builds where stats_init() may not be called)
556   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_stats_lc)) {
557     return; // Mutex not initialized, skip logging
558   }
559 
560   // Now safe to use the mutex
561   mutex_lock(&g_stats_mutex);
562   log_info("Server Statistics:\n"
563            "  frames_captured=%llu\n"
564            "  frames_sent=%llu\n"
565            "  frames_dropped=%llu\n"
566            "  Average FPS: capture=%.2f, send=%.2f",
567            (unsigned long long)g_stats.frames_captured, (unsigned long long)g_stats.frames_sent,
568            (unsigned long long)g_stats.frames_dropped, g_stats.avg_capture_fps, g_stats.avg_send_fps);
569   mutex_unlock(&g_stats_mutex);
570 }




References g_stats, g_stats_mutex,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_info, mutex_lock, and
    mutex_unlock.


Referenced by stats_logger_thread().




void
  stats_cleanup (void )


Cleanup the stats mutex. Uses lifecycle gate to safely destroy the
    stats mutex. Ensures other threads don't attempt to use the mutex after it's
    destroyed.


Definition at line 201 of file stats.c.


201                          {
202   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_stats_lc);
203 }






References lifecycle_shutdown().






int
  stats_init (void )


Initialize the stats mutex.


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

188                      {
189   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_stats_lc, "stats")) {
190     return 0; // Already initialized
191   }
192   return 0;
193 }






References lifecycle_init().


Referenced by server_main().






void
  * stats_logger_thread (void * arg)


Main statistics collection and reporting thread function. This
    background thread performs continuous monitoring of server performance and
    logs comprehensive statistics reports at regular intervals. It operates
    independently of the main server processing threads to avoid performance
    impact.










THREAD
  EXECUTION FLOW: 



  
1.

  
INITIALIZATION:

  
 •

  
Log thread startup (with debug instrumentation)

  
 •

  
Initialize loop counters for debugging

  
 •

  
Set up monitoring infrastructure

  
2.

  
MAIN MONITORING LOOP:

  
 •

  
Check shutdown flag frequently (every 10ms)

  
 •

  
Sleep in small intervals to maintain responsiveness

  
 •

  
Collect statistics from all system components every 30 seconds

  
 •

  
Generate comprehensive performance reports

  
3.

  
STATISTICS COLLECTION:

  
 •

  
Global buffer pool utilization

  
 •

  
Per-client connection and activity status

  
 •

  
Packet queue performance metrics

  
 •

  
Hash table efficiency statistics

  
 •

  
Frame processing counters

  
4.

  
CLEANUP AND EXIT:

  
 •

  
Detect shutdown signal and exit loop gracefully

  
 •

  
Log thread termination for debugging

  
 •

  
Return NULL to indicate clean exit








MONITORING
  METHODOLOGY: 


NON-BLOCKING DATA COLLECTION:


    
  
  • Uses reader locks on shared data structures
    • 
  
  • Takes atomic snapshots of volatile counters
    • 
  
  • Minimal impact on operational performance
    • 
  
  • Safe concurrent access with render threads
    • 



RESPONSIVE SHUTDOWN:


    
  
  • Checks g_should_exit every 10ms during sleep periods
    • 
  
  • Exits monitoring loop immediately when shutdown detected
    • 
  
  • No hanging or delayed shutdown behavior
    • 
  
  • Clean resource cleanup
    • 







PERFORMANCE
  METRICS COLLECTED: 


CLIENT STATISTICS:


    
  
  • Total active clients
    • 
  
  • Clients with audio queues active
    • 
  
  • Clients with video queues active
    • 
  
  • Per-client connection duration
    • 



BUFFER POOL METRICS:


    
  
  • Global allocation/deallocation rates
    • 
  
  • Peak memory usage patterns
    • 
  
  • Buffer pool efficiency
    • 
  
  • Memory fragmentation indicators
    • 



PACKET QUEUE PERFORMANCE:


    
  
  • Per-client enqueue/dequeue rates
    • 
  
  • Packet drop incidents
    • 
  
  • Queue depth histograms
    • 
  
  • Overflow frequency analysis
    • 



HASH TABLE EFFICIENCY:


    
  
  • Client lookup performance
    • 
  
  • Hash collision statistics
    • 
  
  • Load balancing effectiveness
    • 
  
  • Access pattern analysis
    • 







DEBUGGING
  AND TROUBLESHOOTING: 


EXTENSIVE DEBUG LOGGING: The function contains detailed debug
    printf statements for troubleshooting threading issues. This instrumentation
    helps diagnose:


    
  
  • Thread startup/shutdown problems
    • 
  
  • Shutdown detection timing issues
    • 
  
  • Sleep/wake cycle behavior
    • 
  
  • Statistics collection reliability
    • 



DEBUG OUTPUT INCLUDES:


    
  
  • Function entry/exit points
    • 
  
  • Loop iteration counts
    • 
  
  • Shutdown flag state changes
    • 
  
  • Sleep cycle progression
    • 
  
  • Statistics collection timing
    • 







ERROR
  HANDLING: 


    
  
  • Graceful handling of data access failures
    • 
  
  • Continued operation if individual metrics fail
    • 
  
  • No fatal errors that would crash statistics thread
    • 
  
  • Degraded reporting if subsystems unavailable
    • 







PERFORMANCE
  CONSIDERATIONS: 


    
  
  • 30-second reporting interval balances visibility vs overhead
    • 
  
  • 10ms sleep granularity provides responsive shutdown
    • 
  
  • Read-only access minimizes lock contention
    • 
  
  • Background processing doesn't affect real-time performance
    • 



Parameters


arg Thread argument (unused - required by thread
  interface)


Returns


NULL on clean thread termination


Note


This function runs continuously until server shutdown



Debug printf statements help diagnose threading issues




All statistics collection is non-blocking and read-only




Warning


Thread must be properly joined to prevent resource
  leaks


Definition at line 327 of file stats.c.


327                                      {
328   (void)arg;
329 
330   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
331     // Log buffer pool statistics every 10 seconds with fast exit checking (10ms intervals)
332     for (int i = 0; i < 1000 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit); i++) {
333       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms sleep
334     }
335 
336     // Check exit condition before proceeding with statistics logging
337     // Check multiple times to avoid accessing freed resources during shutdown.
338     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
339       break;
340     }
341 
342     // Collect all statistics data first
343 #ifndef NDEBUG
344     char lock_debug_info[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM] = {0};
345     // Check exit condition again before accessing lock_debug.
346     // lock_debug might be destroyed during shutdown
347     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && debug_sync_is_initialized()) {
348       uint64_t total_acquired = 0, total_released = 0;
349       uint32_t currently_held = 0;
350       debug_sync_get_stats(&total_acquired, &total_released, &currently_held);
351 
352       safe_snprintf(lock_debug_info, sizeof(lock_debug_info),
353                     "Historical Mutex/RWLock Statistics:\n"
354                     "  Total locks acquired: %llu\n"
355                     "  Total locks released: %llu\n"
356                     "  Currently held: %u\n"
357                     "  Net locks (acquired - released): %lld",
358                     (unsigned long long)total_acquired, (unsigned long long)total_released, currently_held,
359                     (long long)total_acquired - (long long)total_released);
360     }
361 #endif
362 
363     // Collect client statistics
364     // Check exit condition again before accessing rwlock.
365     // rwlock might be destroyed during shutdown.
366     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
367       break;
368     }
369 
370     rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
371     int active_clients = 0;
372     int clients_with_audio = 0;
373     int clients_with_video = 0;
374     char client_details[BUFFER_SIZE_XLARGE] = {0};
375     int client_details_len = 0;
376 
377     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
378       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
379         active_clients++;
380         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].audio_queue) {
381           clients_with_audio++;
382         }
383         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].outgoing_video_buffer) {
384           clients_with_video++;
385         }
386       }
387     }
388 
389     // Collect per-client details
390     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
391       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
392       bool is_active = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);
393       const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
394 
395       if (is_active && client_id_snapshot != 0) {
396         // Check audio queue stats
397         if (client->audio_queue) {
398           uint64_t enqueued, dequeued, dropped;
399           packet_queue_get_stats(client->audio_queue, &enqueued, &dequeued, &dropped);
400           if (enqueued > 0 || dequeued > 0 || dropped > 0) {
401             int len =
402                 safe_snprintf(client_details + client_details_len, sizeof(client_details) - client_details_len,
403                               "  Client %u audio queue: %llu enqueued, %llu dequeued, %llu dropped", client_id_snapshot,
404                               (unsigned long long)enqueued, (unsigned long long)dequeued, (unsigned long long)dropped);
405             if (len > 0 && client_details_len + len < (int)sizeof(client_details)) {
406               client_details_len += len;
407             }
408           }
409         }
410         // Check video buffer stats
411         if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
412           video_frame_stats_t stats;
413           video_frame_get_stats(client->outgoing_video_buffer, &stats);
414           if (stats.total_frames > 0) {
415             int len = safe_snprintf(client_details + client_details_len, sizeof(client_details) - client_details_len,
416                                     "  Client %u video buffer: %llu frames, %llu dropped (%.1f%% drop rate)",
417                                     client_id_snapshot, (unsigned long long)stats.total_frames,
418                                     (unsigned long long)stats.dropped_frames, stats.drop_rate * 100.0f);
419             if (len > 0 && client_details_len + len < (int)sizeof(client_details)) {
420               client_details_len += len;
421             }
422           }
423         }
424       }
425     }
426     rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
427 
428     // Single comprehensive log statement
429     if (client_details_len > 0) {
430       log_info("Stats: Clients: %d, Audio: %d, Video: %d\n%s", active_clients, clients_with_audio, clients_with_video,
431                client_details);
432     }
433   }
434 
435   log_server_stats();
436 
437   asciichat_error_stats_print();
438 
439   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
440   asciichat_errno_destroy();
441 
442   return NULL;
443 }






References client_info::active,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), asciichat_error_stats_print(),
    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::audio_queue,
    BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM, BUFFER_SIZE_XLARGE,
    client_info::client_id, debug_sync_get_stats(),
    debug_sync_is_initialized(), g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, g_should_exit, log_info,
    log_server_stats(), MAX_CLIENTS,
    client_info::outgoing_video_buffer, packet_queue_get_stats(),
    platform_sleep_us(), rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock,
    safe_snprintf(), stats, US_PER_MS_INT, and
    video_frame_get_stats().


Referenced by server_main().




void
  update_server_stats (void )


Update global server statistics (placeholder). This function is
    intended to update the global server statistics structure with current
    performance metrics. Currently unimplemented but provides the framework for
    centralized statistics updates.










PLANNED
  FUNCTIONALITY: 


    
  
  • Aggregate per-client frame counts into global totals
    • 
  
  • Calculate moving averages for FPS metrics
    • 
  
  • Update system health indicators
    • 
  
  • Compute performance trend data
    • 



IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY:


    
  
  • Thread-safe updates using g_stats_mutex
    • 
  
  • Atomic operations for frequently updated counters
    • 
  
  • Efficient aggregation algorithms
    • 
  
  • Minimal performance impact
    • 



INTEGRATION POINTS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads when updating frame counts
    • 
  
  • Invoked by client management code for connection metrics
    • 
  
  • Used by packet queue systems for throughput tracking
    
    
  
    • 



Todo


Implement comprehensive statistics aggregation


Note


Function currently serves as placeholder for future
  development


Definition at line 473 of file stats.c.


473                                {
474   // Aggregate statistics from all active clients
475   uint64_t total_frames_sent = 0;
476   uint64_t total_frames_dropped = 0;
477 
478   // Read-lock to safely iterate over all clients
479   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
480 
481   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
482     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
483     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
484       // Aggregate frames sent to all clients
485       total_frames_sent += client->frames_sent;
486 
487       // Get dropped frame statistics from outgoing video buffer
488       if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
489         video_frame_stats_t stats;
490         video_frame_get_stats(client->outgoing_video_buffer, &stats);
491         total_frames_dropped += stats.dropped_frames;
492       }
493     }
494   }
495 
496   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
497 
498   // Update global statistics atomically
499   mutex_lock(&g_stats_mutex);
500   g_stats.frames_sent = total_frames_sent;
501   g_stats.frames_dropped = total_frames_dropped;
502 
503   // Calculate frames_captured from frames_sent and frames_dropped
504   // frames_captured = frames_sent + frames_dropped (total output frames)
505   if (total_frames_sent > 0 || total_frames_dropped > 0) {
506     g_stats.frames_captured = total_frames_sent + total_frames_dropped;
507   }
508 
509   mutex_unlock(&g_stats_mutex);
510 }






References client_info::active, atomic_load_bool(),
    client_info::client_id, client_info::frames_sent,
    g_client_manager, g_client_manager_rwlock, g_stats,
    g_stats_mutex, MAX_CLIENTS, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, client_info::outgoing_video_buffer,
    rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock, stats, and
    video_frame_get_stats().






Variable
  Documentation




server_stats_t g_stats [extern]


Global server statistics structure. Maintains aggregated
    performance metrics for the entire server including frame processing rates,
    client counts, and system health indicators. All access to this structure
    must be protected by g_stats_mutex.




CONTENTS:


    
  
  • frames_captured: Total frames received from all clients
    • 
  
  • frames_sent: Total ASCII frames delivered to all clients
    • 
  
  • frames_dropped: Frames lost due to buffer overflows or processing
    delays
    • 
  
  • avg_capture_fps: Moving average of frame capture rate
    • 
  
  • avg_send_fps: Moving average of frame delivery rate
    • 



Definition at line 163 of file stats.c.


163 {0};






Referenced by log_server_stats(), server_main(), and
    update_server_stats().






mutex_t g_stats_mutex [extern]


Mutex protecting global server statistics. Ensures thread-safe
    access to g_stats structure from both the statistics collection thread and
    any operational threads that update counters. Uses standard mutex (not
    reader-writer) due to relatively infrequent access.


Definition at line 172 of file stats.c.


172 {0};






Referenced by log_server_stats(), server_main(), and
    update_server_stats().
